title: Method for performing Raman spectroscopy within a logging while drilling instrument
abstract: A downhole tool has a tool body with an outer diameter equal to a borehole diameter, at least one cavity formed in and opening to an outer surface defining the outer diameter of the tool body, a light source, a filter, and a light detector mounted in the at least one cavity, and a window disposed at the opening of the at least one cavity, wherein the window encloses the cavity.
claims: 1. A method, comprising: cutting a fresh surface in a wall of a borehole extending through a formation with one or more blades of a downhole tool; taking a Raman spectroscopy measurement of the fresh surface from within the borehole extending through the formation before the fresh surface is exposed to borehole fluids with a Raman spectroscopy measurement system, the Raman spectroscopy measurement system including: a light source disposed in a first cavity in an outer surface of a blade of the one or more blades, wherein the first cavity is enclosed by a first window flush with the outer surface of the blade; a filter disposed in a second cavity in the outer surface of the blade, wherein the second cavity is enclosed by a second window flush with the outer surface of the blade; and a light detector disposed in the second cavity; generating a Raman spectrum from the Raman spectroscopy measurement; and determining kerogen maturity in the formation from the Raman spectrum.
